comment: Metal deposit layout blueprint. Deposits are placed on concentric rings;
each ring gets countPerPlayer deposits per player slice, phase-shifted by
sliceOffset (fraction of one slice width). Per ring:
dTerrainLevels — pad height in D-DEPOSIT-bar steps (METAL_DEPOSIT_STEP);
null = ride the natural terrain height under the deposit.
flatPadCells — circular flattened pad diameter in build cells; null =
auto-size to the smallest pad covering the resource footprint.
terrainBlendRadius — world units outside the pad where terrain eases
back to natural with a raised-cosine skirt.
depositCluster — how each ring origin expands into deposits. Two types:
type 'group-ring' (default when omitted): count deposits on a circle
of radius around the origin; angleOffset is radians relative to the
origin's radial angle from map center. Each deposit is a standalone
pad — overlapping pads at different heights cliff (closest wins).
type 'group-manual': one deposit per authored spot, and the group is
ONE TERRAIN UNIT. Spots use one of two addressing forms (never mix):
OFFSET spots {x, y} — world-unit offsets in the origin's radial
frame (+x away from map center, +y perpendicular), so every player's
slice keeps the same local shape. RING-COORDINATE spots
{radiusFraction, sliceOffset} — placed by the exact same oval/slice
formula as a standalone ring, so merging existing rings into one
group keeps their positions identical for every player count and
map size (ring must author countPerPlayer 1). Each spot may author
its own dTerrainLevels (omit = inherit ring, null = natural height)
and its own flatPadCells (omit = inherit ring).
Terrain: every deposit's resource footprint stays perfectly flat at
its own height; the union of the group's flat pads is fully
overridden by one smoothed field that cosine-interpolates between
the plateaus; the whole field eases back to natural terrain over
terrainBlendRadius. Use this when large pads must sit near each
other at different heights without cliffing.
Example: "depositCluster": { "type": "group-manual", "spots":
[ { "x": 0, "y": -230, "dTerrainLevels": 0 },
{ "x": 200, "y": 115, "dTerrainLevels": 2 },

rings.comment: RIGHT ARC — one smoothed terrain group per player; spots are ring coordinates, placed exactly where the four former standalone RIGHT ARC rings were
